segments.text: You walk into a public washroom, shut the door and there it is. The strange gap beneath the door, too wide to ignore
and somehow always there. No matter where you are, malls, airport, schools,
that inch of exposure always follows you. It's awkward. It's vulnerable, but it is intentional. That tiny opening,
it's not just for air flow and cleaning.
It's there to make sure you don't get too comfortable. Even building codes,
they enforces this gap. Not just for access, but to keep things moving. A frictionless timer, a quiet nudge that always says, "Wrap it up." The space
gives you privacy, but only just enough to keep you moving. If you like this,
follow for more such amazing design content.
